sutta: monks possessing 5 qualities a sick man is an ill help to himself but 5 he treats not himself with what is beneficial the first 1 he treats not himself with what is beneficial the second he knows no measure in his treatment third 1 he applies not the medicines fourth 1 he says not he sets not out the extent of his illness to 1 who to 1 who tends him in goodness of heart saying in going it goes thus when it returns it comes so while it is with me it is just us nor is he the kind of man who endures the onset of bodily aches and pains wrecking shooting stabbing bitter galling life taking monks possessing these 5 qualities a sick man is an ill help to himself that's the end of the sutta
commentary: here this is saying such a person is not helpful to himself like when he's sick the first 1 he does not know what is beneficial what is not beneficial the second 1 he does not know measure that means even what is beneficial if he takes too much of it that means he doesn't know the the measure that he should take when even medicine you take too much of it he can be harmful the third 1 he does not apply the medicines when he needs when he should when he should use the medicines he does not use them the fourth 1 he does not tell the person who is tending him about his sickness in detail in in clearly and the fifth 1 he cannot endure uh bodily pains like and this type of person is uh does not help himself much like when he's sick the next

does not tell the person who is tending him about his sickness in detail
